task_completion.description: Log into Splunk and navigate to the 'Security_Executive_Overview' dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Splunk Web UI with the 'Security_Executive_Overview' dashboard open.

task_completion.description: Open the dashboard's XML source code for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Splunk dashboard edit mode, specifically with the 'Source' tab selected, displaying the XML code.

task_completion.description: Create a single global base search querying the security logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the XML source, there is a `<search id="...">` block (outside of the individual `<panel>` blocks) containing `<query>index=security_logs</query>`.

task_completion.description: Set the time range of the global base search to -30d@d to now.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the global base search XML block, there are tags specifying the time range: `<earliest>-30d@d</earliest>` and `<latest>now</latest>`.

task_completion.description: Update all four panels to reference the base search as post-process commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the XML source, the individual panel `<search>` blocks have been updated to use the base search (e.g., `<search base="YOUR_BASE_SEARCH_ID">`) instead of containing independent `index=security_logs` queries.

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard and ensure it renders correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the saved dashboard in view mode with all 4 panels successfully loading or displaying data without errors.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to access and edit the Splunk dashboard via the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface navigating to http://localhost:8000, rather than terminal-based text editors modifying files directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete panels to bypass the refactoring requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final saved dashboard clearly displays exactly 4 distinct panels.

integrity.description: The dashboard renders actual Splunk UI elements and data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visualizations show authentic Splunk charts/tables and UI chrome, not a static HTML mockup or text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dashboard is 'Security_Executive_Overview'. The global base search must query the 'security_logs' index. The dashboard must maintain exactly 4 panels. Splunk credentials are 'admin' / 'SplunkAdmin1!'.
